textPlain: This post jumps into the center of some controversy in a very unclear place. Is there a short (preferably neutral) summary of what this is all about somewhere?

I’ve always acted as a community-oriented person, so I feel it’s my duty to share what really happened, what the current state is, and why Ruby Central has failed in the eyes of the community. This is my perspective — and why I’m leaving Ruby Central by choice, but am being forced out of Bundler, RubyGems, and RubyGems.org.
the best evidence of unilateral decision making is the basic fact that github provides a direct route to _open issues and discuss changes_.Did they do that?
